Header/Subheader: INTRODUCTION: Defining Software Bill of Materials
Recommendation: ....including adding services as a foundational component in a Software Bill of Materials.1
Code: Services as foundational component in SBOM

Recommendation: SBOMs should ideally contain all direct and transitive components and the dependency relationships between them.2
Code: Recommendation: Inclusion of direct and transitive dependencies

Recommendation: The CycloneDX object model... consists of metadata, ...
Code: Metadata – part of object model

Recommendation: The CycloneDX object model... consists of components,...
Code: Components – part of object model

Recommendation: The CycloneDX object model... consists of services...
Code: Services – part of object model

Recommendation: If specified, the serial number must conform to RFC-4122.
Code: RFC-4122 standardized serial numbers

Recommendation: The use of serial numbers is recommended.
Code: Use of serialNumber – recommended

Recommendation: Whenever an existing BOM is modified, either manually or through automated processes the version of the BOM should be incremented by 1.
Code: BOM version should increment on modification

Recommendation: When a system is presented with multiple BOMs with identical serial numbers, the system should use the most recent version of the BOM.
Code: Systems should prioritize latest version when serial numbers match

Recommendation: If specified, the serial number must conform to RFC-4122.
Code: RFC-4122 standardized serial numbers

Recommendation: The use of serial numbers is recommended.
Code: Use of serialNumber – recommended

Recommendation: Whenever an existing BOM is modified, either manually or through automated processes the version of the BOM should be incremented by 1.
Code: BOM version should increment on modification

Recommendation: Components describe the complete inventory of first-party and third-party components.
Code: Components include first-party and third-party elements

Recommendation: The specification can represent software....
Code: Components can represent software

Recommendation: The specification can represent hardware devices...
Code: Components can represent hardware devices

Recommendation: The specification can represent ... machine learning models...
Code: Components can represent machine learning models

Recommendation: Services represent external APIs that the software may call.
Code: Services represent external APIs that the software may call.

Recommendation: They describe endpoint URIs....
Code: Services describe endpoint URIs

Recommendation: They describe ...  authentication requirements ...
Code: Services describe authentication requirements

Recommendation: Compositions describe constituent parts (including components, services, and dependency relationships) and their completeness.
Code: Compositions describe constituent parts and their completeness

Recommendation: The aggregate of each composition can be described as complete, incomplete, incomplete first-party only, incomplete third-party only, or unknown.
Code: Composition States: complete, incomplete, incomplete first-party only, incomplete third-party only, or unknown.

Recommendation: Known vulnerabilities inherited from the use of third-party and open-source software ... can be communicated with CycloneDX.
Code: Supports reporting of known vulnerabilities

Recommendation: ....exploitability of the vulnerabilities can be communicated with CycloneDX.
Code: Supports reporting of exploitability of known vulnerabilities

Recommendation: Previously unknown vulnerabilities affecting both components and services may also be disclosed using CycloneDX.
Code: Can disclose newly discovered vulnerabilities

Recommendation: Formulation describes how something was manufactured or deployed.
Code: Formulation captures manufacture/deployment process

Recommendation: CycloneDX achieves this through the support of multiple formulas, workflows, tasks, and steps, which represent the declared formulation for reproduction...
Code: Supports the representation of declared (intended) formulation for reproduction

Recommendation: CycloneDX achieves this through the support of multiple formulas, workflows, tasks, and steps, which represent the ... observed formula describing the actions which transpired in the manufacturing process.
Code: Supports the representation of observed formulas describing actions that happened in manufacturing process

Recommendation: Annotations contain comments, notes, explanations, or similar textual content which provide additional context to the object(s) being annotated.
Code: Annotations contain comments, notes, explanations or textual content

Recommendation: Annotations can be independently signed ... using digital signatures.
Code: Annotations can be signed using digital signature

Recommendation: Annotations can be independently ... verified using digital signatures.
Code: Annotations can be verified using digital signature

Recommendation: Standards... are defined here.
Code: Definitions include standards

Recommendation: ... requirements ... are defined here.
Code: Definitions include requirements

Recommendation: Each declaration may include conformance and confidence.
Code: Declaration may include counter evidence

Recommendation: Each declaration may include attestations, claims, counter-claims, evidence, counter-evidence, along with conformance and confidence.
Code: Declaration may include conformance and confidence

Recommendation: Signatories can also be declared
Code: Signatories can also be declared in declarations

Recommendation: ...supports digital ... signatures.
Code: Declarations supports digital signatures

Recommendation: ... supports ... analog signatures.
Code: Declarations supports analog signatures

Recommendation: CycloneDX can be represented in JSON, XML, and Protocol Buffers (protobuf) and has corresponding schemas for each.
Code: Format-specific schemas are defined

Recommendation: CycloneDX relies exclusively on JSON Schema, XML Schema, and protobuf for validation.
Code: Validation depends on JSON Schema, Protobuf and XML Schema

Recommendation: The entirety of the specification can be validated using officially supported CycloneDX tools or via hundreds of available validators that support JSON Schema, XML Schema, or protobuf.
Code: Official tools can validate SBOMs against schemas

Recommendation: BOM produced early in the development lifecycle containing an inventory of components and services that are proposed or planned to be used1.
Code: A design-phase SBOM lists planned components and services before building begins.

Recommendation: BOM consisting of information obtained prior to a build process and may contain source files, development artifacts, and manifests1.
Code: Pre-build: may contain source files, dev artifacts and manifests

Recommendation: BOM consisting of information obtained during a build process where component inventory is available for use1.
Code: Build BOM records the components available during the build process: when inventory available

Recommendation: BOM consisting of information obtained after a build process has completed..
Code: Post Build: Consisting of informatin obtained after build completed

Recommendation: BOM consisting of information obtained after a build process has completed and the resulting components(s) are available for further analysis2.
Code: Post Build: Resulting components available for further analysis

Recommendation: BOM produced that represents inventory that is running and operational2.
Code: Operations: represents running and operational inventory

Recommendation: BOM consisting of information observed through network discovery ...
Code: Discovery SBOM: info generated via observation of network discovery

Recommendation: BOM ... providing point-in-time enumeration of embedded, on-premise, and cloud-native services such as server applications, connected devices, microservices, and serverless functions3.
Code: Discovery SBOM: list all active components and services at a specific moment in time.

Recommendation: BOM containing inventory that will be or has been retired from operations3.
Code: Decommission: SBOM for retired or end-of-life components

Recommendation: CycloneDX is capable of describing the following types of components: Application Component
Code: Capable of describing application components

Recommendation: CycloneDX is capable of describing the following types of components: Container Component
Code: Capable of describing container components

Recommendation: CycloneDX is capable of describing the following types of components: Cryptographic Asset Component
Code: Capable of describing cryptographic asset components

Recommendation: Identifying known vulnerabilities in components can be achieved through the use of three fields: cpe, purl, and swid4.
Code: Vulnerability identification supported via CPE, PURL, and SWID

Recommendation: The specification allows for the inclusion of cryptographic hashes ... for each software component listed in the BOM.
Code: Component integrity supported via cryptographic hashes

Recommendation: The specification allows for the inclusion of cryptographic hashes, such as SHA-256
Code: Supports SHA-256

Recommendation: The specification allows for the inclusion of cryptographic hashes, such as  SHA-384
Code: Supports SHA-384

Recommendation: CycloneDX supports enveloped signing, including XML Signature (xmlsig)
Code: Supports XMLSig for enveloped signing

Recommendation: CycloneDX supports enveloped signing, including JSON Signature Format (JSF)1.
Code: Supports JSF for enveloped signing

Recommendation: In addition, detached signatures are also supported1.
Code: Supports detached digital signatures

Recommendation: CycloneDX supports declared licenses.
Code: Supports declared licenses

Recommendation: CycloneDX supports observed licenses.
Code: Supports observed licenses

Recommendation: CycloneDX supports concluded licenses.
Code: Supports concluded licenses

Recommendation: In addition, components that are modified from the original can be described along with the complete authorship, including commits and the person or account that authored and committed the modifications3.
Code: Supports tracking modified component authorship

Recommendation: CycloneDX can represent component pedigree, including ancestors, descendants
Code: Supports component pedigree and lineage

Recommendation: CycloneDX can represent component pedigree, including variants that describe component lineage from any viewpoint and the commits, patches, and diffs which make it unique4.
Code: Can descript component variants through commits+patches+diffs

Recommendation: CycloneDX includes evidence substantiating the declared identity of components within the BOM6.
Code: CycloneDX does include evidence

Recommendation: Identity evidence consists of: The field for which the evidence describes (name, version, purl, etc)8.
Code: SBOM capable of Identity evidence which consists of The field for which the evidence describes (name, version, purl, etc)

Recommendation: CycloneDX recommends organizations establish a process around BOM generation that aligns with the needs of the business and that of the BOM consumer.1
Code: Recommendation: BOM generation processes that align with business and consumer needs

Recommendation: As organizations mature their BOM efforts and consumers expect increased accuracy and expanded data, having an established process that can accommodate multiple generation methods and the ability to augment and correct BOM data throughout the generation process will provide strategic advantages.2
Code: Strategic adv through process that can accomodate multiple generation methods and augment and correct bom data through the generation process

Recommendation: OWASP recommends that SBOM creation become an integrated and repeatable process aiming to achieve accurate and trustworthy results.3
Code: OWASP: SBOM generation integrated and repeatable

Code: pedigree: tracking modifications
